calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-26-2018 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-29-2018 Set For Trial On Nov-13-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Hearing On Order To Show Cause Re Preliniary Injunction
Off Calendar Mar-26-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Adrian Driscoll. Esq. 415-788-1900 of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeny for Plaintiff Young Chi.
Lisa Berkowitz, Deputy City Attorney 915-554-3825 Office of the City Attorney for Defendant CCSF.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares his views. Plaintiff argues on the merits, Morgado. Defendant argues Morgado case, Doyle & Butler case.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes)

Taken under submission. Defendant submits a proposed order.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-30-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Although a writ of administrative mandamus is the only procedural mechanism available to challenge the Police Commission's termination decision, the complaint also alleges that the California Public Safety Officers' Procedural Bill of Rights Act ("POBRA") was violated because Plaintiff was not provided with the opportunity for an administrative appeal. Cal. Gov. code sec. 3309.5 provides that "[t]he superior court shall have initial jurisdiction over any proceeding brought by any public safety officer against any public safety department for alleged violations of this chapter." Plaintiff is given leave to amend to specifically identify the provision of POBRA that provides for an administrative appeal of a police commission's termination decision. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg
